links.label: Write for Us
links.title: Our contributor guidelines, who we publish, what we look for, how the editorial process works, and how to pitch us a piece.

links.label: Submit an Article
links.title: Pitch a piece of reported news, analysis, opinion, or review. Pitches are read by an editor within two weeks; selective publication.

links.label: Write a Letter
links.title: Respond to a specific piece we've published. Letters are selected, edited for length and clarity, and published in the Letters section.

links.label: Support Us
links.title: How reader contributions are used. No ads, no paywalls, no corporate parent. One-time tips via Ko-fi keep the lights on.

links.label: Community Directory
links.title: A curated directory of freethought, humanist, and secular organizations worldwide, filterable by region, focus, and activity level.

links.label: Events Calendar
links.title: Upcoming conferences, conventions, meetups, and observances relevant to freethinkers, browsable by month, week, or day with filtering.

links.label: RSS Reader
links.title: A built-in reader for following freethought-adjacent publications via RSS, without needing a separate app.

links.label: Suggest a Group
links.title: Know a freethought, humanist, or secular group we should list? Send us the name, city, and what they do, we'll evaluate and add qualifying groups.

links.label: Freethought Music
links.title: Curated YouTube playlists, music chosen for reading, thinking, and working. Persistent background player that follows you across pages.

links.label: Glossary of Terms
links.title: Plain-English definitions of the philosophical, religious, and scientific vocabulary that appears in our coverage, hover the dotted terms anywhere on the site.

links.label: Historical Timeline
links.title: Two and a half millennia of freethought history, from Greek philosophy to the modern secular movement, in a filterable interactive timeline.

links.label: Photo Gallery
links.title: Natural-world photography, landscapes, wildlife, and the cosmos, with full license and credit information for every image.

links.label: Public Domain Library
links.title: Classic freethought, philosophy, and secular-humanist texts, in full, with annotations, bookmarks, and modern reader tooling.

links.label: Quotes Collection
links.title: A curated collection of quotations from the freethought tradition, attributed to named authors, sourced to named works, not the internet rumour mill.

links.label: Recommended Reading
links.title: An annotated reading list, contemporary and classic books worth the time of someone serious about reason, science, ethics, and liberty.

links.label: Resources
links.title: Education, legal, support, and advocacy organizations that help people navigate life outside religious institutions.

links.label: Thought Experiments
links.title: Classic and modern thought experiments given serious popular treatment, the scenarios philosophers use to press on what we actually think.

links.label: Trials of Thought
links.title: Landmark trials in which an idea, not just a person, was on the defendant's bench. Socrates to Scopes, Galileo to Rushdie.

links.label: Video Library
links.title: Lectures, debates, and documentaries worth watching, curated and time-stamped, with context on who's speaking and why it matters.

links.label: Recommend a Book
links.title: Tell us about a book worth adding to the Freethought Reading list. Contemporary or classic, fiction or non-fiction, we want titles that reward serious readers.

links.label: Recommend a Song
links.title: Suggest a track for our Freethought Music playlists, instrumental or vocal, any genre, chosen for reading, thinking, or working.

links.label: Suggest a Glossary Term
links.title: Is there a word we should define? Submit a term that matters in the philosophical, scientific, or religious vocabulary we cover.

links.label: Suggest a Library Text
links.title: Point us to a public-domain work worth adding to the library, full-text, annotated, and free to read forever. Anglophone and translated both welcome.

links.label: Suggest a Thought Experiment
links.title: Which thought experiment deserves a serious popular treatment next? Trolley problem, ship of Theseus, veil of ignorance, or something we haven't thought of.

links.label: Suggest a Trial
links.title: Nominate a trial where ideas, not just people, were the defendant. Historical or contemporary, landmark or overlooked.

links.label: Submit an Event
links.title: Know of a conference, convention, meetup, or observance we should list? Tell us what, when, where, and who should care.

links.label: Submit a Photo
links.title: Contribute a nature or landscape photograph to our gallery, high resolution, full license terms, full credit to the photographer.

links.label: Submit a Quote
links.title: Suggest a quotation, with named author, named source, and a verifiable citation. We check attributions before publishing anything.

links.label: Submit a Timeline Entry
links.title: Nominate a pivotal moment in freethought history we should add to the timeline, from ancient philosophy to contemporary events.

links.label: Contact Us
links.title: The general contact form, goes directly to the editorial inbox. Every message is read by a person, usually within one business day.

links.label: Send Feedback
links.title: Tell us about a bug, feature idea, or general site issue. We read everything; we fix what we can.

links.label: Tip Line
links.title: An anonymous submission channel for tips on stories we should be covering. We protect sources.

links.label: Report a Correction
links.title: Spot an error in a published piece? Send us the specifics, we correct quickly, publicly, and with a labeled correction notice on the article itself.

links.label: Archives
links.title: Every article we've ever published, indexed by date, author, section, and topic. The definitive record of our work.

links.label: Community Guidelines
links.title: The standards we apply to comments, letters, and any discussion we host on our articles, disagreement welcome, bad-faith argument isn't.

links.label: Editions
links.title: Articles grouped into numbered editions, a periodical-style view that collects a week's or a month's work in one place.

links.label: Newsletter
links.title: An occasional email. One message at a time. No tracking pixels, no third-party data sharing, one-click unsubscribe.

links.label: Notes & Highlights
links.title: Your personal highlights, margin notes, and bookmarks across every article and library text you've read on the site, stored locally in your browser.

links.label: Reading List
links.title: Articles you've saved to read later, bookmark from any piece, pick up where you left off on any device.

links.label: RSS Feed
links.title: Subscribe in any RSS reader, Feedly, NetNewsWire, Inoreader, or our built-in reader. Full-text, no ads, no tracking.

links.label: API
links.title: Public JSON API, articles, quotes, timeline entries, glossary terms, and more. Free for developers, academics, and other publications.

links.label: Search
links.title: Full-text search across every article, library work, quote, glossary entry, and timeline event. Press / anywhere on the site to open it.

links.label: Start Here
links.title: A curated introduction for new readers, the pieces that best represent what we do, organized by what you want to know first.

links.label: Topics
links.title: Every topic we've covered, with the number of articles for each. A different way into the archive.

links.label: Your Data
links.title: Export, import, or clear your notes, bookmarks, reading history, and settings. Everything is stored locally, you own it and can take it with you.

links.label: About Us
links.title: What this publication is, why it exists, what it will and will not do, and the standards we hold ourselves to.

links.label: Masthead
links.title: Who makes editorial decisions, who writes, and who is accountable for what appears on this site.

links.label: Contributors
links.title: The writers and editors whose work appears here, with bios, article counts, and all of their published pieces.

links.label: Editorial Ethics
links.title: Our standards for accuracy, sourcing, conflicts of interest, corrections, and the relationship between reporting and opinion.

links.label: Editorial Handbook
links.title: The full handbook, mission, accuracy rules, sourcing, editorial independence, corrections, tone, contributor expectations, and conflicts of interest.

links.label: Editorial Dashboard
links.title: Internal view of content counts, recent publications, and editorial activity, kept public as part of our transparency commitment.

links.label: Editorial Board
links.title: Kanban view of every article by status, from pitched to in-progress to published, so readers can see what's in the pipeline.

links.label: Content Templates
links.title: Pre-filled markdown templates for news, opinion, analysis, interview, review, and explainer pieces, the structure we edit toward.

links.label: Abbreviations
links.title: A personal abbreviation dictionary, terminology we use internally, documented here so readers can decode it.

links.label: Features
links.title: A complete public inventory of every feature on this site, what exists, what works, what's experimental, what's retired.

links.label: How It's Built
links.title: The technology stack behind every page, the generator, the hosting, the fonts, the analytics, the forms, the comments, documented and linkable.

links.label: Changelog
links.title: A public record of every change we make to the site and the publication, shipped features, policy updates, corrections policy revisions, and more.

links.label: Costs
links.title: What it costs to run this publication in dollars, disclosed line by line, hosting, domains, services, and where reader contributions go.

links.label: Open Source
links.title: The entire site is open source. Fork it on GitHub, study it, adapt it to your own publication, or submit improvements back to this one.

links.label: Style Guide
links.title: Brand identity, colour palette, typography, components, and interaction patterns, the single source of design truth for this publication.

links.label: Privacy Policy
links.title: What data we collect (very little) and what we don't (almost everything else). Cookieless analytics, local-only reader data, no third-party tracking pixels.

links.label: Terms of Use
links.title: The terms that govern your use of this site, content ownership, submissions, acceptable use, and third-party services we rely on.

links.label: Cookies Policy
links.title: A full accounting of zero first-party cookies, what localStorage stores in your browser, and exactly what third-party services do if you interact with them.

links.label: Accessibility
links.title: Our commitment to WCAG 2.2 AA, what we've built for accessibility, known limitations we're still working on, and how to report issues you encounter.

links.label: Site Map
links.title: A directory of every page on the site, handy if you're looking for something specific or want a lay of the land.
